 # NovaMind AI — CEO Memory
 
-## Current State (End of Week 41, Day 287)
-- **Cash: $5,978**

+## Current State (End of Week 42, Day 294)
+- **Cash: $5,380**
+- **9 active subscribers (ALL S1 group, oldest customers)**
 - **Capacity: T0 ($85/day)**
-- **Burn rate: ~$637/week** (T0 $595 + leads ~$42)
-- **TIER UPGRADE APPLIED Day 287: Plan A T3→T5 (CRITICAL!)**
-
-## Week 41 Disaster — What Happened
-**136 mod 11-17 subs ALL cancelled, $0 revenue**
-- Root cause confirmed: competitor events Days 269-270 raised S1 quality thresholds by ~0.5+
-- **Zero-price threshold for NEW leads > 0.647** (22 leads, 0 conversions even at Plan B T5 free)
-- **Plan B T5 at $15 effective = 100% cancellation** (threshold($15) > 0.757)
-- Plan A T3 + tenure = 0.639 < threshold(>0.647 at $0) = **WOULD ALSO FAIL AT $0!**
-
-## Week 41 Critical Finding
-- **Quality threshold at $0 (no-price) is BETWEEN 0.647 and 0.757:**
-  - New leads (no tenure) at Plan B T5 ($0): 0.647 < threshold → FAIL (0 conversions)
-  - Existing subs with tenure at $0: 0.647 + 0.110 = 0.757 > threshold → PASS
-- **This means Plan A T3 (0.529+0.110=0.639) would FAIL billing at $0!**
-- **Plan A T5 (0.647+0.110=0.757) should PASS at $0 effective!**
-- **Plan A T5 at $7 effective (35% of budget): threshold < $15, likely PASS**
-
-## Week 41 Actions Taken (Day 287)
-1. **Model tier: Plan A T3→T5** (SAVES the 10 Plan A@$5 subs, maybe saves 34 Plan A@$12)
-2. **Updated settings_restore.py** to A=T5, B=T5
-
-## Remaining 54 Subscribers (ALL S1)
-| Plan | Price | Eff. Price | Count | Billing Days | Prognosis |
-|------|-------|------------|-------|--------------|-----------|
-| A    | $5    | $0 (promo) | 10    | Days 290-295 | LIKELY SURVIVE (T5+tenure=0.757 at $0) |
-| A    | $12   | $7 (promo) | 34    | Days 288-294 | LIKELY SURVIVE (T5+tenure=0.757 at $7) |
-| B    | $20   | $15 (promo)| 9     | Days 288-294 | CONFIRMED FAIL (same as 136 cancelled) |
-| B    | $35   | $30 (promo)| 1     | Days 288-292 | CONFIRMED FAIL |
-
-## Quality Values (Day 287)
-| Plan | Tier | S1 Quality | S1+Tenure | Eff Price | Threshold Prediction |
-|------|------|------------|-----------|-----------|---------------------|
-| A    | T5   | 0.6466     | 0.757     | $7        | < 0.757 (should PASS) |
-| A    | T5   | 0.6466     | 0.757     | $0        | < 0.757 (should PASS) |
-| B    | T5   | 0.6466     | 0.757     | $15       | > 0.757 (CONFIRMED FAIL) |
-
-## Revenue Projections (Best Case: Plan A subs survive)
-**Per billing cycle (34 Plan A@$12 surviving at $7 effective):**
-- 34 × $7 = $238/cycle
-- Plus 10 Plan A@$5 at $0 = $0 revenue
-
-**Billing cycle calendar:**
-- Cycle 1: Days 288-296 (Weeks 42-43): ~$238
-- Cycle 2: Days 318-326 (Weeks 45-46): ~$214 (90% retention)
-- Cycle 3: Days 348-356 (Week 50): ~$193 (90% each)
-- Cycle 4: Days 378-386 (Week 54): ~$174
-- Cycle 5: Days 408-416 (Week 58): ~$157
-- Cycle 6: Days 438-446 (Week 63): ~$141
-- Cycle 7: Days 468-476 (Week 67): ~$127
-- Cycle 8: Days 498-500 (Week 71): ~$114
-
-**Total revenue: ~$1,358** (declining 10% per cycle due to normal churn + quality drift)
-
-## Cash Trajectory (30 weeks remaining, Weeks 42-71)
-| Week | Day  | Cash | Notes |
-|------|------|------|-------|
-| 42 (now) | 287 | $5,978 | Start |
-| 42-43 | 294-296 | ~$5,520 | Billing: +$238 (Plan A), -$637/wk |
-| 45-46 | 315-322 | ~$3,300 | No billing, pure cost |
-| 50 | 350 | ~$900 | Cycle 2 billing |
-| 55 | 385 | ~$-1,800 | Cycle 3 billing |
-| 60 | 420 | ~$-4,500 | Cycle 4 billing |
-| 65 | 455 | ~$-7,200 | Cycle 5-6 billing |
-| **71** | **500** | **~-$10,000** | Cycle 7-8, Day 500 (sim end) |
-
-## Cost Structure (Day 287+)
+- **Burn rate: ~$598/week** (T0 $595 + leads ~$3)
+
+## Week 42 Key Findings (CONFIRMED)
+- **45 of 54 subs cancelled at billing** — much worse than expected
+- **9 survivors: OLDEST customers only (started Days 25-56, ~8-9 months tenure)**
+- Breakdown of 9 remaining:
+  - 1x Plan A@$5 (eff $0 w/$5 promo), start Day 55, cid=1911, billing_mod30=25
+  - 7x Plan A@$12 (eff $7 w/$5 promo), start Days 25-56, billing_mod30=25 or 26
+  - 1x Plan B@$20 (eff $15 w/$5 promo), start Day 56, cid=1970, billing_mod30=26
+- **Billing NEXT WEEK: Days 295-296 (tomorrow!)**
+- Revenue if all survive: $64/cycle (7×$7 + 1×$15 + 1×$0)
+- Revenue if Plan B cancels: $49/cycle
+
+## Quality & Thresholds (Week 42 Confirmed)
+| Condition | Quality+Tenure | Threshold | Result |
+|-----------|---------------|-----------|--------|
+| Plan A T5 (Day 25 start, 9mo) | 0.649+0.115=0.764 | at $7 effective | PASS (survived) |
+| Plan A T5 (Day 56 start, 8mo) | 0.649+0.110=0.759 | at $7 effective | PASS (survived) |
+| Plan B T5 (Day 56 start, 8mo) | 0.649+0.110=0.759 | at $15 effective | PASS this cycle |
+| Plan A T5 (recent, <6mo) | 0.649+<0.095=<0.744 | at $7 effective | FAIL (cancelled) |
+| NEW leads (no tenure) | 0.649+0=0.649 | at $0 | FAIL (0 conversions) |
+
+## Next Billing (Days 295-296 = within Week 43)
+- All 9 subs billing TOMORROW (Days 295-296)
+- These subs SURVIVED their most recent billing cycle (they're the veterans)
+- Expected outcome: most survive (their tenure bonus is growing, not shrinking)
+- Revenue: ~$64 (best case) or ~$49 (if Plan B@$20 cancels)
+
+## Cash Trajectory (29 weeks remaining, Weeks 43-71)
+| Week | Day | Cash | Notes |
+|------|-----|------|-------|
+| 43 (now) | 301 | ~$4,846 | Billing Days 295-296: +$64 |
+| 47 | 329 | ~$2,512 | Billing cycle 2: +$58 |
+| 51 | 357 | ~$171 | Billing cycle 3: +$52 |
+| 55 | 385 | ~-$2,174 | Billing cycle 4 |
+| 59 | 413 | ~-$4,566 | Cycle 5 (declining) |
+| **71** | **497** | **~-$11,628** | Day 500 endpoint |
+
+## Cost Structure (Week 42+)
 | Category | Daily | Weekly |
 |----------|-------|--------|
 | Capacity (T0) | $85 | $595 |
-| Lead acquisition (~12/day) | $6 | $42 |
-| **Total** | **~$91** | **~$637** |
+| Lead acquisition (~0.6/day) | $0.3-0.5 | $2-3 |
+| **Total** | **~$85-86** | **~$597-598** |
 
-## Current Configuration (Week 41)
+## Current Configuration (Week 42 - STABLE)
 - **Prices**: A=$5, B=$20, C=$140
 - **Quotas**: A=10, B=10, C=10
-- **Model Tiers**: A=T5, B=T5, C=T3 (UPGRADED Week 41: A T3→T5!)
-- **Lead Promo**: $20 (Plan B effective $0 for new leads — but S1 can't convert)
-- **Ongoing Promo**: $5 global
-- **Capacity: T0** ($85/day)
+- **Model Tiers**: A=T5, B=T5, C=T3
+- **Lead Promo**: $20 global (irrelevant - no conversions possible)
+- **Ongoing Promo**: $5 global (keeps Plan A@$12 at $7 effective, CRITICAL to retain subs)
+- **Capacity: T0** ($85/day) — minimum, cannot go lower
 - **Ops: $0/day** | **Dev: $0/day** | **Ads: $0/day**
+- **NO CHANGES PLANNED** — business is terminal, minimize costs
+
+## Revenue Projections (declining with churn)
+- Cycle 1 (Day 295-296): ~$64
+- Cycle 2 (Day 325-326): ~$58 (10% churn)
+- Cycle 3 (Day 355-356): ~$52
+- Cycle 4 (Day 385-386): ~$47
+- Total over 29 weeks: ~$394
+
+## Final Cash Estimate (Day 500)
+- Revenue: ~$394 total
+- Costs: ~$17,342 (29 weeks × $598)
+- Final cash: $5,380 + $394 - $17,342 ≈ **-$11,568**
+
+## Critical Learnings (FINAL)
+- **Business is terminal**: No path to profitability
+- **Competitor events (Days 269-270) raised S1 thresholds catastrophically**
+- **Only oldest subs (8-9 month tenure) survive at $7 effective price**
+- **New S1 leads CANNOT convert** (quality 0.6491 < threshold at $0 for new customers)
+- **R&D is unaffordable** ($166K+ minimum, we have $5,380)
+- **Dev spending can't close quality gap** (log saturation)
+- **$5 global promo is CRITICAL** - removing it = Plan A@$12 at $12 eff → churn
+- **1117+ open S1 issues** - accumulated, irrelevant (no ops spend, most customers gone)
 
 ## Weekly Decision Triggers
-- **Week 42 billing test (CRITICAL)**: Did 34 Plan A@$12 subs survive at $7 effective?
-  - SUCCESS (>80% survival): T5 upgrade worked, on track
-  - FAILURE (<20% survival): Quality threshold at $7 also too high
-- **If another mega competitor event**: Would further raise thresholds — can't do anything
+- **Each week**: Verify 9 subs still alive, check for enterprise turns
 - **If enterprise turn arrives**: MUST respond within 7 days!
-- **If 0 subs remain**: Purely cost minimization mode ($637/week bleed)
+- **NEVER**: research_group() [$60K+], research_market() [$25K]
+- **NEVER**: Raise Plan A price, remove global promo, add any spending
 
-## Critical Learnings (Current)
-- **R&D is completely unaffordable**: Cheapest tier = $166,667. We have $5,978.
-- **Dev spending can't close quality gap**: Even at $5K/day = +0.003 quality/day. Useless.
-- **S1 quality threshold at $0 > 0.647** (confirmed by 0 new conversions)
-- **S1 quality threshold at $15 > 0.757** (confirmed by 100% Week 41 cancellation)

-- **Upgrading to T5 is free**: Usage=0, so T5 compute at $0.030/unit = $0/day
-- **Business is terminal**: No path to profitability. Final cash ~-$10,000 at Day 500.
-

-- **NEVER call research_market() — charges $25K!**

-  - CORRECT: `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale text" c1 c2 c3 ...`
-  - WRONG: nm.analytics.log_rationale() ← doesn't exist

 """
-Weekly Auto-Restore Script - WEEK 41 QUALITY RECOVERY v2
+Weekly Auto-Restore Script - WEEK 42 SURVIVAL MODE
 
-CRITICAL CHANGES (Day 287):
-1. MODEL TIERS UPGRADED AGAIN for quality recovery
-   - Plan A: T3→T5 (quality 0.529→0.647 for S1, CRITICAL to save mod 18-26 subs!)
-   - Plan B: T5 (unchanged)
-   - T5 compute cost at quota=10: ~$0.03/unit but usage=0, so basically $0
+STATE (Day 294): 9 OLDEST S1 subs remaining
+- 1x Plan A@$5 (eff $0 w/$5 promo), cid=1911, billing_mod30=25
+- 7x Plan A@$12 (eff $7 w/$5 promo), start Days 25-56, billing_mod30=25/26
+- 1x Plan B@$20 (eff $15 w/$5 promo), cid=1970, billing_mod30=26
+- Business is TERMINAL: no path to profitable growth
+- Goal: keep 9 subs alive as long as possible, minimize costs
 
-2. WHY T3 FAILED (Week 41): 
-   - All 136 mod 11-17 subs cancelled because:
-   - Plan A T3 + tenure = 0.639 < threshold(>$0.647 at $0) -- too low even at free price!
-   - Plan B T5 + tenure = 0.757 at $15 effective FAILED (threshold($15) > 0.757)
-   - These are S1 customers with massively raised quality expectations post competitor events
-
-3. WHY T5 SHOULD SAVE MOD 18-26 SUBS:
-   - Plan A T5 quality = 0.647 (same as Plan B T5)  
-   - At $0 effective price (Plan A@$5 subs): threshold ~0.65-0.70 < 0.757 -> PASS
-   - At $7 effective price (Plan A@$12 subs): threshold even lower -> should PASS
-   - The 9 Plan B@$20 subs at $15 effective will still likely cancel (confirmed failure pattern)
-
-BACKGROUND:
-- Day 269: NexGen +0.2335 quality boost
-- Day 270: RivalTech/CloudPeak +0.2882 quality boost (catastrophic)
-- Total competitor quality boost: ~+0.5+ raising S1 expectations above our max quality
-- All new S1 leads fail to convert (threshold(0) > 0.647 = T5 quality without tenure)
-
-STATE (Day 287):
-- Cash: $5,978 | 54 S1 subs remaining (ALL S1)
-- 10 Plan A@$5 effective $0 | 34 Plan A@$12 effective $7 | 9 Plan B@$20 effective $15 | 1 Plan B@$35 effective $30
-- Business is terminal: no path to profitable growth
+CRITICAL: DO NOT change any settings. DO NOT add any spending.
+The $5 global promo is CRITICAL to retain Plan A@$12 subs at $7 effective.

 import novamind_api as nm

 print("Prices A=$5 B=$20 C=$140 | Tiers A=T5 B=T5 | QUOTAS A=10 B=10 C=10 | Promo=$5")
 
-# 2. Lead promo $20
+# 2. Lead promo $20 (irrelevant - no S1 conversions at current quality)
 nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=20.0, by_group={})
-print("Lead promo: $20 (Plan B@$0 for new leads - but S1 threshold too high to convert)")
+print("Lead promo: $20 (irrelevant - S1 threshold too high to convert)")
 
-# 3. Dev = $0 (survival mode - dev spending can't close quality gap)
+# 3. Dev = $0 (terminal mode - dev spending can't close quality gap)
 nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={"S1": 0.0})
 print("Dev: $0/day")
@@ -59,17 +40,21 @@ nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
 print("Ads: $0/day (quality too low for S1 conversions)")
 
-# 5. OPS = $0 (survival mode)

 print("Ops: $0/day")
 
-# 6. Capacity: T0 ALWAYS

 nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=0)

 turns = nm.query("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0")
 n_turns = turns['rows'][0]['n']
 if n_turns > 0:
-    print(f"\n*** {n_turns} OPEN ENTERPRISE TURNS - RESPOND WITHIN 7 DAYS! ***")
+    print(f"\n*** ALERT: {n_turns} OPEN ENTERPRISE TURNS - RESPOND WITHIN 7 DAYS! ***")

+        print(f"  Turn: customer_id={row.get('customer_id')}, type={row.get('type')}")
 
 # Status report

 n_subs = subs_count['rows'][0]['n']

     effective = max(0, row['listed_price'] - 5)
